commit.message:
feat: initial implementation of zbx — Zabbix configuration as code CLI

Add a production-ready Python CLI tool that manages Zabbix monitoring
configuration as YAML files deployable through a Git workflow.

## Architecture

zbx/
  models.py          — Pydantic v2 models: Template, Item, Trigger,
                        DiscoveryRule, ItemPrototype. All Zabbix API integer
                        IDs are mapped bidirectionally through typed enums
                        (TriggerSeverity, ItemType, ItemValueType).

  config_loader.py   — Loads YAML files (single file or recursive directory
                        scan). Validates every document against the Pydantic
                        schema and surfaces clear error messages on failure.

  zabbix_client.py   — Thin JSON-RPC HTTP client built on requests. Handles
                        authentication (Zabbix 5.4+ and legacy user.login),
                        context-manager login/logout, and full CRUD for
                        templates, items, triggers, discovery rules and item
                        prototypes. Includes find_templates() for the export
                        command.

  diff_engine.py     — Computes a structured TemplateDiff between the
                        desired YAML state and the current Zabbix state.
                        Produces ChangeType-tagged ResourceChange objects
                        with FieldChange lists (old_value → new_value) for
                        items, triggers and discovery rules.

  deployer.py        — Applies diffs via zabbix_client. Supports dry-run
                        mode. Never auto-deletes resources — removals emit a
                        warning and require manual action (safe default).
                        All operations are idempotent.

  formatter.py       — Rich-based Terraform-style output: green + for adds,
                        yellow ~ for modifications, red - for removals.
                        Includes plan summary and apply result table.

  commands/
    apply.py         — Shows plan, prompts for confirmation, applies with
                        progress spinner. Flags: --dry-run, --auto-approve.
    plan.py          — Dry-run diff against live Zabbix.
    diff.py          — Alias of plan; matches infra-engineer muscle memory.
    validate.py      — Schema validation without API connection.
    export.py        — Fetches a template from Zabbix and serialises it to
                        YAML (migration path from web UI to Git).

  cli.py             — Typer app. Registers all five commands. Global
                        --verbose/-v flag configures RichHandler logging.
                        --version/-V prints the package version.

## Example configs

configs/templates/linux-observability.yaml
  6 items (CPU, memory, load, processes, uptime)
  4 triggers (high CPU disaster/warning, memory average/high)
  2 LLD rules (filesystem discovery, network interface discovery)
  with item prototypes for each

configs/templates/nginx.yaml
  4 items (status page, active connections, request rate, process count)
  2 triggers (process down → disaster, high request rate → warning)

## Workflow

  zbx validate configs/          # validate YAML schema
  zbx plan     configs/          # dry-run: show what would change
  zbx diff     configs/          # diff local config ↔ live Zabbix
  zbx apply    configs/          # apply with confirmation prompt
  zbx apply    configs/ -y       # apply non-interactively (CI/CD)
  zbx export   linux-server      # export existing template to YAML

## Configuration

Connection settings are read from environment variables or a .env file:
  ZBX_URL, ZBX_USER, ZBX_PASSWORD, ZBX_VERIFY_SSL, ZBX_TIMEOUT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add RecommendedReaderVersion advisory mechanism to cDAC RuntimeInfo contract (#125029)

…ontract

Adds a new CDAC_RECOMMENDED_READER_VERSION constant and
RecommendedReaderVersion global to datadescriptor.inc. The runtime team
can increment this value when making changes where updating diagnostic
tools is recommended. This is purely advisory and does not necessarily
mean a breaking change occurred.

The RuntimeInfo contract exposes two new properties:
- CurrentReaderVersion: a compile-time constant representing the reader
version this contract was authored against (currently 1)
- RecommendedReaderVersion: reads the runtime global of the same name

Diagnostic tools can compare these to determine whether to recommend an
update to the user. When the runtime ships a higher
RecommendedReaderVersion than a tool's CurrentReaderVersion, the tool
knows newer contracts are available.

---------

commit.message:
Fix test race: serialize bundle tests sharing static state

JsBundleServiceTests and JsBundleServiceCompressionTests both write to
the static _bundles ConcurrentDictionary via BuildBundle(). xUnit runs
different test classes in parallel, so the compression tests (which write
500 'x' chars as theme-switcher.js) could overwrite the bundle between
BuildBundle and TryServeAsync in BuildBundle_MinifiesNonMinFiles.

Fix: add [Collection] attributes so tests sharing static bundle state
run sequentially. Same fix applied to CssBundleService test classes.

Fixes #864

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent webhook panic on auto-generated vLLM presets with empty GPU params (#1824)

## Problem

For auto-generated vLLM presets (any HuggingFace model not in KAITO's
built-in preset list, e.g. `Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B`), `GPUCountRequirement` is
always an empty string because the generator has no logic to derive GPU
count from HuggingFace metadata. Calling `resource.MustParse("")` on
this empty string caused the webhook to panic with:

```
cannot parse '': quantities must match the regular expression...
```

This crashes the webhook and returns a 500 error to the user on
workspace creation.

## Root Cause

- Built-in presets hardcode both `GPUCountRequirement` and
`TotalSafeTensorFileSize`
- Auto-generated vLLM presets only populate `TotalSafeTensorFileSize`
(from HuggingFace model metadata) but never set `GPUCountRequirement`
- The validation code called `resource.MustParse()` on both fields
without checking for empty strings

## Fix

- **Independent field checks**: Instead of a single all-or-nothing OR
guard, check each field independently. GPU count validation is skipped
only when `GPUCountRequirement` is empty. GPU memory + distributed
inference checks are skipped only when `TotalSafeTensorFileSize` is
empty.
- **Safe parsing**: Replace `resource.MustParse` with
`resource.ParseQuantity` with proper error handling to prevent panics on
malformed input.
- **Both API versions**: Applied to both `v1alpha1` and `v1beta1`
workspace validation.
- **Warning logs**: Log warnings when skipping individual checks due to
empty or unparseable fields.

## Testing

Deployed the fix and validated that creating a workspace with
`Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B` (a non-built-in model) no longer panics the webhook.

commit.message:
fix: nil concat crash on empty date and jobstart chunk buffering

ui.lua: comment.createdAt:match() can return nil when createdAt is ""
(our fallback value). Concatenating nil crashes with a Lua error.
Guard both the empty-string case and the nil match result.

github.lua: jobstart delivers stdout in chunks where the last element
of each chunk is a partial line joined to the first element of the next
chunk. The previous code dropped these boundaries, corrupting large JSON
responses (e.g. PRs with many comments). Rewritten with a running
string buffer that correctly reconstructs the full output.

commit.message:
Add ENABLE_CK build option for CK-free builds (#2074)

* Add ENABLE_CK build option for CK-free builds

Add ENABLE_CK environment variable (default=1) to control whether
Composable Kernel is required at build time. When ENABLE_CK=0:

- setup.py: skip 3rdparty/composable_kernel copying, skip CK assertion,
  auto-exclude CK-dependent modules via core._get_ck_exclude_modules()
- aiter/jit/core.py: add _get_ck_exclude_modules() that identifies CK-dependent
  modules through config pattern matching and a hardcoded exclusion set (V3 ASM
  modules exempted since they build with shim headers). build_module() conditionally
  adds CK include paths only when CK_3RDPARTY_DIR exists, sets -DAITER_CK_FREE=1
  compiler flag, and filters include paths to existing directories only. Also fix
  SystemExit -> RuntimeError for build failures (SystemExit is uncatchable by
  normal exception handlers).
- csrc/include/hip_compat.h: guard ck_tile/core.hpp include behind !AITER_CK_FREE,
  define WARP_SIZE=64 independently when CK-free

This is the foundation for CK-free builds that reduce build time from ~35min
to ~8min by compiling only Triton kernels + C++ bindings.

Ref: https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/issues/2073

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

commit.message:
test: add comprehensive HBP and Moza wheelbase report tests (#126)

Add protocol-level integration tests for both microcrates:

HBP (hbp_protocol_tests.rs):
- Encoding/decoding roundtrips for all 3 layouts (prefixed, raw 2-byte, raw 3-byte)
- Sequential axis value decoding and monotonicity
- Timeout detection via None returns on empty/truncated reports
- Overflow/reset edge cases (u16::MAX, zero)
- Concurrent/interleaved layout parsing independence
- Normalization boundary precision and monotonicity
- parse_axis sliding window and usize::MAX safety
- Layout discrimination edge cases
- Byte-order (LE) verification
- Proptest fuzzing (1024 cases): arbitrary reports, roundtrips, OOB safety

Moza wheelbase report (moza_report_tests.rs):
- Full field roundtrip (steering, throttle, brake, clutch, handbrake, buttons, hat, funky, rotary)
- Known-good sequences for Moza R9/R12/R16/R21 style reports
- LE byte-order verification for all axis fields
- Report ID acceptance/rejection for all 256 values
- Malformed report handling (empty, truncated, wrong ID, partial axes)
- Zero-fill behavior for partial buttons, rotary, optional axes
- RawWheelbaseReport accessor edge cases
- parse_axis boundary values and OOB safety
- Derive trait smoke tests (Copy, Eq, Debug)
- Constant offset consistency checks
- Proptest fuzzing (1024 cases): per-field roundtrips, arbitrary lengths, OOB safety

All tests use Result-returning style (no unwrap/expect per project rules).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
